S12.notes: Single-phase poll on 17 November 2023; counting on 3 December 2023. BJP won 163 of 230 seats. Ingested from the legacy /full-statistical-reports/ static URL template (no /api/election-result entry).

S16.notes: Single-phase poll on 7 November 2023; counting on 4 December 2023 (delayed one day at state request to avoid Sunday). Zoram People's Movement (ZPM) won 27 of 40 seats, ending MNF rule. Ingested from the legacy /full-statistical-reports/ static URL template.

S26.notes: Two-phase poll: 20 seats on 7 November 2023, remaining 70 seats on 17 November 2023; counting on 3 December 2023. BJP won 54 of 90 seats, ending Congress's single term. Ingested from the legacy /full-statistical-reports/ static URL template.

S29.notes: Single-phase poll on 30 November 2023; counting on 3 December 2023. Indian National Congress won 64 of 119 seats, defeating BRS (39) which had governed since the state's 2014 creation. Ingested from the legacy /full-statistical-reports/ static URL template.

Delhi is a Union Territory with a Legislative Assembly. The Assembly's powers are limited (Article 239AA): public order, police, and land are reserved to the Lieutenant Governor.

Puducherry is a Union Territory with a 30-seat elected Legislative Assembly plus 3 LG-nominated members.

U08.notes: First elected government in J&K since 2018. The state was reorganised into a Union Territory on 31 October 2019; this Assembly's powers under Article 239A and the J&K Reorganisation Act 2019 are narrower than a full state's.
